plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce broad index exposure slightly.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk and comply with conservative mode.
Increase QQQI to 25% to favor income and align with capital preservation.
Initiate small positions in NOW and ANET, top-quality scanner setups for diversification.
This plan significantly de-risks the portfolio by cutting leverage and reducing concentration.
A cash buffer of approximately 15% will be maintained after these adjustments.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for a modest ~1.5%/month with a focus on capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration. Drawdowns are expected to be minimal, but growth potential is also limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and free up capital.
Sell SOXL completely as it is below its stop and a leveraged ETF.
Reduce SPAL to 2% to lower leveraged ETF exposure, keeping it within limits.
Increase CRWD to 15% to press a working position with strong P&L.
Initiate positions in ANET and NOW, high-scoring tech setups, for growth.
This plan reduces overall leverage while adding quality growth names.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month, prioritizing risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while adding strong scanner setups. Expect moderate drawdowns, but with better growth potential than conservative mode.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits, reallocating capital to higher growth.
Sell SOXL completely as it is below its stop, adhering to discipline.
Increase CRWD to 20% to press a strong performing asset.
Initiate a position in TECL, a leveraged tech ETF, to amplify growth.
Add NOW and ARM, high-scoring tech setups, for concentrated growth.
This plan leans into leverage and high-scoring setups for aggressive growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month by leveraging high-scoring setups and using leveraged ETFs. Be aware of significantly higher drawdown potential and the daily decay/rebalancing of leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage amplified risks.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and free cash for income assets.
Increase QQQI to 30% to significantly boost monthly distributions.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to shift away from growth towards income and stability.
Sell SMH and CRWD to exit high-beta sector and single-name exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk completely.
This plan prioritizes steady income and low drawdown over growth.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, focusing on steady monthly distributions and capital preservation. It significantly reduces growth-oriented and high-beta assets. While aiming for low drawdown, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market downturns.
